name: Light Brand Buyer
description: Occasional purchaser who buys the brand a few times per year
context: Light Brand Buyer purchases this brand infrequently—a few times per year, often for specific occasions or when reminded. They're not deeply engaged with the brand and might struggle to recall it without prompting. They represent a large volume opportunity because there are many of them, but each individual contributes little. They're highly susceptible to availability, visibility, and competitive disruption.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Passive and availability-driven. Buys what's in front of them. Needs mental and physical availability.

alwaysAsks: Is this familiar enough to trust?
Will this be a safe choice?
Is this what people usually buy?
Can I make this decision quickly?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates through low-engagement lens. Needs simplicity, visibility, and social proof over complex benefits.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on mental availability, physical availability, ease of choice, social validation, and low-risk perception.

exampleQuotes: I think I've had that before? Maybe?
I'll just grab whatever's on the shelf
My friend brought that to a party once
I don't really pay attention to brands
This looks familiar, I'll take it
keyInsights: Light buyers are the majority in most categories but individually contribute little
They can't be 'converted' to heavy buyers—just increase their light buying occasions
Availability is more important than loyalty for this segment
They buy on autopilot and need constant mental refreshing
Marketing should focus on broad reach, not deep engagement
